rulings.Case Title: BRIAN UGALE VS. NICHOLAS ROOMEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants' Discovery Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One), And Document Production Request (Set One), And Document Production Request (Set One), And Request For Monetary Sanctions, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And Declaration Of Richard L. Daniels, Esq.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for March 25, 2025, line 1. PLAINTIFFS BRIAN UGALE, STEVEN LAUER, BRENT LANGLOIS'S Motion To Compel Defendants' Discovery Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One), And Document Production Request (Set One), And Document Production Request (Set One), And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiffs' Motion to Compel Defendants' Discovery Responses and Request for Monetary Sanctions is transferred to department 301 to be heard on April 7, 2025. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 5407 DIAMOND HEIGHTS HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, A VS. HILDELISA JOHNSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for March 25, 2025, line 6. DEFENDANT HILDELISA JOHNSON'S MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant's Motion to Strike Amended Complaint is DENIED, 10 days to answer. Complaint properly states a cause of action for breach of the CC&Rs against the Defendant (not the decedent). Factual issues raised in the argument regarding the 2nd and 3rd causes of action are not proper on a motion to strike. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: GAETANI REAL ESTATE VS. SUMMER SALIBA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, If Entered
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for March 25, 2025, line 8. DEFENDANT SUMMER SALIBA'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, If Entered. Defendant's Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment is DENIED. Defendant's motion is effectively a motion for reconsideration of the motion to vacate that was previously denied. The present motion does not meet the standard of CCP 1008 because it is not based on new or different facts, circumstances, or law which were not known at the time of the previous motion to vacate. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
